title: Features
description: Everything Atelier does, and nothing it doesn't.

blocks.text: Everything you need. Nothing you don't.

blocks.body: Atelier is a kanban board for people who find most tools too loud. It has exactly the features a focused workflow needs.

left.title: Three-column board
left.body: Every project starts with To do, Working on, and Completed. Click any column title to rename it — make it match how you actually think, not how some PM textbook does.

right.title: Multiple projects
right.body: Projects live as icon glyphs in the left rail. Add a new one with + at the bottom. Each project has its own name, icon, and accent colour so you can tell them apart at a glance.

left.title: Cards that move
left.body: Drag cards between columns or reorder within a list. Drop them anywhere — Atelier figures out the right position. Use ⌘↵ to save a new card, Esc to cancel.

right.title: Clear done with one click
right.body: Finished a sprint? The Completed column has a clear button that removes all done cards at once. No hunting through a list, just a clean slate.

blocks.heading: Appearance
blocks.body: Atelier looks right in any light.

items.title: Light / Dark / System
items.body: Pick a fixed mode or follow the OS. Switching is instant — no reload, no flicker.

items.title: Accent colours
items.body: Eight accent colours to pick from. Used on buttons, highlights, and interactive elements throughout the app.

items.title: Density
items.body: Comfortable or Compact. Comfortable gives cards more room to breathe; Compact fits more on screen.

blocks.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
blocks.body: Everything has a shortcut. None of them are surprising.

left.title: Truly local-first
left.body: Your data lives in ~/Library/Application Support/com.jude.atelier/atelier-state.json. No account, no server, no subscription. Quit mid-write and nothing is lost — Atelier writes a timestamped backup to localStorage on every change, and on launch it picks whichever copy is newer.

right.title: No network needed
right.body: Fonts ship inside the app via Fontsource. Once downloaded, Atelier needs zero network access to run. It also auto-checks for updates silently and only surfaces one if it finds something new.
